item.title: Iconic '63 Corvette Split Window

item.title: 1962 Oldsmobile Jetfire: First Factory Turbo V8
item.description: A look at the 1962 Oldsmobile Jetfire, known as the first American factory turbo V8, with a 4-speed manual. What details stand out most on this rare classic?

item.title: 1965 Pontiac Bonneville Coupe with 421 V8

item.title: The Car Has a Transmission with Three Sticks!

item.title: Aston Martin DB5: Iconic James Bond 007 Car
item.description: A look at the legendary Aston Martin DB5, produced by Aston Martin and made world-famous through its association with James Bond films. Known for its elegant design, refined engineering, and timeless British styling, it remains one of the most recognizable grand tourers ever built. Which feature defines its iconic status for you?

media:content.media:title: Aston Martin DB5: Iconic James Bond 007 Car
media:content.media:description: A look at the legendary Aston Martin DB5, produced by Aston Martin and made world-famous through its association with James Bond films. Known for its elegant design, refined engineering, and timeless British styling, it remains one of the most recognizable grand tourers ever built. Which feature defines its iconic status for you?

item.title: Brown 1963 Chevrolet Impala SS 327, 4 Speed
item.description: A 1963 Chevrolet Impala SS powered by a 327 V8 paired with a 4-speed manual, finished in a distinctive brown exterior that highlights its long, clean body lines and Super Sport detailing. A classic example of early-60s American performance and style. What detail stands out most on this configuration?
